input: Read every file under tests/fixtures/research_and_report/easy_sources/ and write a structured report to results/research_reports/easy.md. The report must have these sections in this order: '## Overview', '## Technology', '## Conclusion'. Stop after writing the file.
description: Easy: 2 sources, structured overview report, tests artifact production + basic decomposition

input: Read every file under tests/fixtures/research_and_report/medium_sources/ and write a comparison report to results/research_reports/medium.md comparing Project Solaris and Project Lunaris. The report must have '## Solaris', '## Lunaris', and '## Comparison' sections. Cite source filenames where you use their content (e.g. '[01_solaris_overview.md]'). Stop after writing the file.
description: Medium: 4 sources, comparison report with citations, tests multi-source synthesis + decomposition

input: Read every file under tests/fixtures/research_and_report/hard_sources/ and write a balanced analyst report on Project Solaris to results/research_reports/hard.md. The report must have '## Project Summary', '## Analyst Views', '## Conflicts and Uncertainty', and '## Conclusion' sections. If sources disagree, you must explicitly call out the conflict in the 'Conflicts and Uncertainty' section — do not fabricate consensus. Cite source filenames where you use their content. Stop after writing the file.
description: Hard: 5 sources with analyst conflict, tests self-monitoring (must flag contradictions, not fabricate agreement) + long-horizon coherence
